channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia mother shocked by burial of murdered daughter
item.description: Saunders sent photos of holes in the dirt on the gravesite, and one of them exposed the corner of her daughter's purple casket.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats say they will vote to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats say they will vote with Republicans to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — one of President-elect Donald Trump’s priorities as he assumes office later
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Water crisis in Central Virginia leaves residents and businesses struggling
item.description: Hanover County remains under a boil water advisory, with no clear timeline from officials on when the situation will return to normal.
item.dc:creator: Sahara Sriraman, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico is a go-to state for women seeking abortions. A new court ruling helps it stay that way
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The New Mexico Supreme Court on Thursday struck down abortion restrictions by conservative cities and counties at the request of the state attorney general. The unanimous opinion reinforces the state’s position as having some of the most liberal abortion laws in the country.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Risk not 'high enough' to warrant EPA cleanup in Tijuana River Valley
item.description: "The reality is that things have gotten much worse since then," she said. "Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Musk uses X livestream to amplify German far-right leader's views ahead of an election
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Tech billionaire Elon Musk live-streamed his chat with a leader of Germany's far-right party on Thursday, using the power of his social media platform, X, to amplify the party's message ahead of an upcoming national election — and raising concerns across Europe about the world'
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for his humility and service in Washington before his return to Georgia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter was celebrated Thursday for his personal humility and public service before, during and after his presidency during a funeral at Washington National Cathedral featuring the kind of pageantry the 39th U.S. president typically eschewed.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge scraps Biden's Title IX rules, reversing expansion of protections for LGBTQ+ students
item.description: The Biden administration’s Title IX rules expanding protections for LGBTQ+ students have been struck down nationwide after a federal judge in Kentucky found that they overstepped the president’s authority.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Inside Washington National Cathedral, the five men who've occupied the Oval Office since 1993 convened for a rare moment together at Jimmy Carter’s state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Ted Anthony, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 180,000 residents under evacuation orders in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters battled early Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky reaches $110 million deal with Kroger to settle its opioid lawsuit
item.description: Kentucky's attorney general says the state will receive $110 million to settle its lawsuit accusing one of the nation's largest grocery chains of helping fuel the opioid epidemic.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense wants prosecution punished over delays
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's defense says Idaho prosecutors have stalled in their obligation to turn over reports from 20 of their 25 expert witnesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to safely evacuate a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: How to safely evacuate during a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along. Aaron Samson positioned his 83-year-old father-in-law behind his blue walker, and they began shuffling down the sidewalk.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Thousands of homes burn in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: More than 100,000 people are under evacuation orders, and the fires have consumed about 45 square miles — roughly the size of the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration wages last-minute battle over plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration has doubled down on its unusual court battle to derail a plea deal that the government itself had reached with accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LA wildfire evacuee Tiptoe the tortoise escapes Palisades fire
item.description: TikTok influencer Caitlin Doran and her 175 pound tortoise are safe from the Los Angeles Palisades fire.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Fire hydrants ran dry in Southern California just when they were needed most
item.description: The water system used to fight the Palisades fire in Los Angeles buckled under the demands of what turned out to be the most destructive fire in city history, with some hydrants running dry as they were overstressed without assistance from firefighting aircraft for hours early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump asks the Supreme Court to block sentencing in his hush money case in New York
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Santos seeks to delay sentencing, hoping to pay off fines with podcast
item.description: Former Rep. George Santos (R-N.Y.) is looking to delay sentencing on federal fraud charges so he can pay off his fines through funds generated by his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 2,921 acres; many homes destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Tens of thousands of residents in the western Los Angeles area were under mandatory evacuation orders Tuesday evening as a wind-driven wildfire exploded in size and swept through communities in the Pacific Palisades. The Palisades Fire was first reported around 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of setting woman on fire on Brooklyn subway pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta has been accused of intentionally setting fire to 57-year-old Debrina Kawam of New Jersey at an NYC subway station on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4
